title: Token Bucket Rate Limiter
instruction: Implement a TokenBucket class in Python. It must: accept capacity (int) and refill_rate (float tokens/second) at construction; expose a consume(tokens=1) -> bool method that returns True if tokens were available and consumed, False otherwise; be thread-safe; and refill tokens over time at the given rate without exceeding capacity. No external dependencies.

test_commands:
python3 -c "
import time
from solution_0 import TokenBucket

# basic consume
bucket = TokenBucket(capacity=5, refill_rate=1.0)
assert bucket.consume(3) == True
assert bucket.consume(3) == False  # not enough tokens
print('basic consume: PASS')

# full capacity respected
bucket2 = TokenBucket(capacity=3, refill_rate=100.0)
assert bucket2.consume(3) == True
assert bucket2.consume(1) == False
time.sleep(0.05)  # 100 t/s * 0.05s = 5 tokens, but cap is 3
assert bucket2.consume(3) == True
print('capacity cap: PASS')

print('ALL PASS')
"
